author: w3Lb
content: WHY: rg ships GUIDE.md + FAQ.md; ripsed's README is good but there's no task-oriented guide, and the README never mentions sd — the incumbent 'sed in Rust' — so the positioning question every evaluator asks ('why not sd?') goes unanswered. SCOPE: (1) GUIDE.md: task-oriented walkthroughs — bulk rename across a repo, multi-step .rip refactor, agent JSON integration end-to-end (request/response/undo), undo workflows, config file. (2) FAQ.md: why literal-by-default (vs rg regex-default), exit codes (after #100), how binary/encoding handling works, lock files explained (the *.ripsed.lock sentinels confuse people — see #84 gitignore addition), 64MiB cap rationale. (3) README comparison section: honest table vs sd and GNU sed — recursion, gitignore-awareness, undo, atomic writes, JSON mode, script files; link benchmarks when #99 lands. Keep claims verifiable; no benchmark numbers until #99.
